story.iWant: to upload multiple files at once via drag-and-drop or file picker
story.soThat: I can efficiently convert batches of presets without repeatedly selecting individual files

tasks.task: Style Drop Zone with Visual Feedback (AC-1)
Implement File Input and Multiple Selection (AC-2)
Create File Card Component (AC-4, AC-5, AC-6)

criterion.title: Large Drop Zone with Visual Feedback
criterion.description: Large drop zone displayed on landing page with clear instructions and visual feedback on drag-over and drop
criterion.verification: - Drop zone minimum 400x300px on desktop - Clear instructions: "Drag & drop preset files here, or click to browse" - Visual feedback on drag-over: Border highlight (dashed → solid), subtle scale animation (1.0 → 1.02), background color change (light gray → light blue tint) - Visual feedback on drop: Brief success animation, drop zone collapses to compact mode - Drop zone styling with dashed border, centered upload cloud/folder icon

criterion.title: Multiple File Selection Support
criterion.description: File picker supports multiple file selection for all supported formats
criterion.verification: - File picker supports multiple selection (input type="file" multiple) - Accept all supported formats: .np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate, .costyle, .dcp - No file size limit (client-side processing handles large files) - Users can select 1-100+ files at once - File picker triggered by clicking drop zone or "Browse Files" button

criterion.title: File Type Validation
criterion.description: Validate file extensions on upload and reject unsupported files with clear error messages
criterion.verification: - Supported: .np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate, .costyle, .dcp (case-insensitive) - Unsupported files rejected with clear error message: "Unsupported file type: example.jpg. Supported formats: NP3, XMP, lrtemplate, .costyle, DCP" - Error displayed inline below drop zone (red text, warning icon) - Mixed batch uploads allowed: Accept supported files, display error for unsupported - Example: 8 supported + 2 unsupported → "8 files uploaded, 2 files rejected"

criterion.title: File Cards Grid Layout
criterion.description: Display uploaded files as individual cards in responsive grid layout
criterion.verification: - Desktop (1024px+): 3 columns - Tablet (768px-1023px): 2 columns - Mobile (less than 768px): 1 column (stacked) - Each file card shows: Filename (truncated if greater than 30 characters, full name on hover), Detected format badge, File size (KB/MB, human-readable), Conversion status (icon: queued, processing, complete, error) - Card styling: White background, subtle shadow, rounded corners (8px border-radius), hover effect (shadow darkens, slight scale)

criterion.title: Format Detection from Filename
criterion.description: Detect format from file extension and display format badge immediately
criterion.verification: - .np3 → NP3 badge (yellow #FFC107) - .xmp → XMP badge (blue #0073E6) - .lrtemplate → lrtemplate badge (magenta #D81B60) - .costyle → Capture One badge (purple #9C27B0) - .dcp → DCP badge (green #4CAF50) - Display format badge immediately on upload (before file reading) - Badge matches colors from Story 10-1 badge system (reuse CSS classes) - Badge visible in file card header (top-right or next to filename)

criterion.title: File Size Display
criterion.description: Calculate and display file size for each uploaded file in human-readable format
criterion.verification: - less than 1 KB: "500 bytes" - less than 1 MB: "345 KB" - greater than or equal to 1 MB: "1.2 MB", "15.8 MB" - File size displayed in file card (below filename or in footer) - No file size limit enforced (client-side WASM handles large files)

criterion.title: Remove File Functionality
criterion.description: Each file card has "Remove" button that deletes file from upload queue
criterion.verification: - Remove button (X icon or "Remove" text link) on each card - Clicking "Remove" deletes file from upload queue: File card removed with fade-out animation, File data cleared from memory, Grid layout reflows automatically - Remove button visible on hover or always visible (mobile: always visible) - Confirm removal for files in "processing" state (prevent accidental loss)

criterion.title: Empty State Handling
criterion.description: Show drop zone with instructions when no files uploaded
criterion.verification: - When no files uploaded, show drop zone with instructions - When all files removed, drop zone reappears (return to initial state) - Empty state message: "No files uploaded yet. Drag & drop files to get started." - Empty state includes visual cue (upload icon, friendly illustration)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: File Upload Data Model with UploadedFile typedef (file, id, format, status, data, convertedData, targetFormat, error), BatchProgress typedef (total, completed, failed, queued), Format Badge Component with BEM naming

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: UploadManager module (js/upload.js) responsible for batch upload, drag-drop handling with inputs (File[]) and outputs (Uploaded file cards). File card grid with responsive breakpoints (3-col desktop, 2-col tablet, 1-col mobile)

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: File Upload Workflow: User drags files → Browser FileList → For each file: Read as ArrayBuffer, Detect format via extension + magic bytes, Create UploadedFile object (status: 'queued'), Append file card to grid

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: AC-3: Batch File Upload with Drag-and-Drop - Large drop zone, visual feedback on drag-over, support multiple file selection, accept all supported formats, reject unsupported files with clear error, display uploaded files as individual cards in grid layout

doc.title: Product Requirements Document - Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: FR-3.3: Batch File Upload with Drag-and-Drop - Large drop zone on landing page, visual feedback on drag-over (highlight, scale animation), support multiple file selection via file picker, accept all supported formats, reject unsupported files with clear error message, no file size limit (client-side processing), display uploaded files as individual cards in grid layout

doc.title: Product Requirements Document - Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: File Upload & Conversion Flow: Drop zone highlights on drag-over with subtle scale animation, multiple files create individual cards (batch mode), each card shows filename, detected format badge, file size, status

doc.title: Architecture - Recipe Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: Architecture Components: web/js/upload.js (Drag-drop zone, batch file handling, progress tracking), web/js/utils.js (Format detection, file validation, error messaging). Format badges color-coded by format type. Responsive breakpoints: Mobile less than 768px (single column, tap-to-browse), Tablet 768-1024px (two-column grid), Desktop greater than 1024px (three-column grid)

doc.title: Architecture - Recipe Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: CSS Variables Color Palette with format badge colors (NP3=#FFC107, XMP=#0073E6, lrtemplate=#D81B60, Capture One=#9C27B0, DCP=#4CAF50). Responsive Breakpoints using mobile-first approach with media queries at 768px (tablet) and 1024px (desktop). BEM Naming convention for CSS classes (.block, .block__element, .block--modifier)

file.symbol: Landing page structure with existing hero section
file.reason: Current landing page HTML to be extended with upload section - contains existing hero, format badges, privacy sections that should be preserved
Existing stylesheet that will be modularized into main.css, components.css, layout.css - need to understand current styling patterns and extract reusable components
Existing app initialization pattern - will need to integrate with new UploadManager module, maintains event-driven architecture
Current file upload implementation - will be refactored into new upload.js module with batch support and drag-drop functionality
Existing format detection (magic bytes, extension matching) - will be integrated into new UploadManager for instant format badge display
Existing error messaging patterns - will be reused for file validation errors (unsupported file types, mixed batch uploads)
Existing WASM conversion interface - no changes needed, but UploadManager will integrate with this for batch conversion in future story

package.name: vanilla-javascript
package.usage: No framework - modern JavaScript with modules for upload manager, drag-drop handling

package.name: browser-file-api
package.usage: FileReader, Blob, FileList for file upload and reading

package.name: drag-drop-api
package.usage: dragover, dragleave, drop events for drag-and-drop zone

package.name: css-grid
package.usage: Responsive grid layout for file cards (3-col, 2-col, 1-col)

package.name: css-flexbox
package.usage: File card internal layout (header, body, footer)

package.name: css-variables
package.usage: Format badge colors and design tokens

constraints.constraint: Vanilla JavaScript only - no React, Vue, Svelte, or other frameworks. Maintain existing web/ directory structure and patterns
Basic HTML/CSS must work without JavaScript - drop zone visible and file picker functional without JS
Extension-based validation only (fast, instant feedback) - content-based validation deferred to conversion step
Accept supported files and reject unsupported files separately - show clear summary (e.g., "8 files uploaded, 2 files rejected")
Support Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+ - no IE11 support needed. Drag-drop may not work on all mobile browsers (fallback to file picker)
Touch targets must be minimum 44x44px on mobile per Apple/Google guidelines. Keyboard navigation for file picker (no keyboard for drag-drop)
Mobile-first CSS approach - base styles for mobile (less than 768px), media queries for tablet (768-1024px) and desktop (1024px+)
BEM naming convention for CSS classes (.block__element--modifier) - follow existing pattern from story 10-1
File card creation must be efficient for 100+ files - batch DOM updates if needed, no memory leaks
Clear, actionable error messages for users - "Unsupported file type: example.jpg" not "Error 0x04F2"

interface.name: UploadManager Class
interface.signature: export class UploadManager { constructor() // Initialize DOM references, event listeners initEventListeners() // Drag-drop, file picker events handleFiles(fileList) // Process uploaded files validateFile(file) // Check extension, return validation result addFileCard(file) // Create and append file card to grid createFileCard(file) // Generate file card HTML removeFile(fileId) // Remove file from grid and memory detectFormat(filename) // Extract format from extension formatFileSize(bytes) // Human-readable file size truncateFilename(name, max) // Shorten long filenames showError(rejected) // Display error for unsupported files showEmptyState() // Show drop zone when no files }

interface.name: File Card HTML Structure
interface.signature:
{filename} {FORMAT}
{fileSize}
{statusIcon} {statusText}

interface.name: Drop Zone HTML
interface.signature:
Drag & drop preset files here

or click to browse
interface.path: web/index.html (to be added after hero section)

interface.name: File Validation Result

tests.standards: Epic 10 focuses on frontend UI/UX with manual testing emphasis. Testing standards: Browser compatibility testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge), Responsive design testing at defined breakpoints (320px, 768px, 1024px), Accessibility testing with keyboard navigation (Tab, Enter, Esc), Device testing on real devices (iPhone, Android, iPad), Visual regression testing for file cards and drag-drop interactions, Performance testing for 100+ file batches
tests.locations: Manual testing documented in story completion notes. Browser compatibility: BrowserStack or manual testing. Responsive testing: Chrome DevTools device emulation + real devices. Accessibility testing: Keyboard navigation, touch target sizes. Performance testing: Chrome DevTools Performance tab for 100+ file batches

test.idea: Manual: Verify drop zone displays correctly with minimum 400x300px dimensions. Test clear instructions visible ("Drag & drop preset files here, or click to browse")
Interaction: Drag file over drop zone → verify border changes (dashed to solid), background color changes (light gray to light blue), subtle scale animation (1.0 to 1.02)
Interaction: Drop file → verify brief success animation, drop zone collapses to compact mode
Visual: Verify dashed border styling, centered upload cloud icon visible
Functional: Click drop zone → verify file picker opens with multiple selection enabled
Functional: Click "Browse Files" button → verify file picker opens
Functional: Select 10 files (Ctrl+Click or Shift+Click) → verify all 10 files uploaded
Functional: Verify file picker accept attribute includes all formats (.np3, .xmp, .lrtemplate, .costyle, .dcp)
Performance: Upload 100 files → verify no memory issues, UI remains responsive
Validation: Upload supported file (.np3) → verify file accepted, card appears
Validation: Upload unsupported file (.jpg) → verify file rejected, error message shown ("Unsupported file type: example.jpg. Supported formats: NP3, XMP, lrtemplate, .costyle, DCP")
Validation: Upload mixed batch (8 supported + 2 unsupported) → verify 8 cards appear, error shows "8 files uploaded, 2 files rejected"
Validation: Test case-insensitive extension detection (.NP3, .Np3, .np3 all valid)
Visual: Verify error displayed inline below drop zone with red text and warning icon
Responsive: Test desktop (1920px) → verify 3-column grid layout
Responsive: Test tablet (768px) → verify 2-column grid layout
Responsive: Test mobile (375px) → verify 1-column stacked layout
Visual: Verify each file card shows filename, format badge, file size, status icon
Visual: Verify card styling (white background, subtle shadow, rounded corners 8px, hover effect with shadow darkening and slight scale)
Interaction: Hover over card → verify shadow darkens, card scales slightly
Functional: Upload .np3 file → verify NP3 badge displayed immediately (yellow #FFC107)
Functional: Upload .xmp file → verify XMP badge displayed immediately (blue #0073E6)
Functional: Upload .lrtemplate file → verify lrtemplate badge displayed immediately (magenta #D81B60)
Functional: Upload .costyle file → verify Capture One badge displayed immediately (purple #9C27B0)
Functional: Upload .dcp file → verify DCP badge displayed immediately (green #4CAF50)
Visual: Verify badge matches colors from Story 10-1 badge system (reuse CSS classes .badge, .badge--np3, etc.)
Visual: Verify badge visible in file card header (top-right or next to filename)
Functional: Upload file <1 KB (500 bytes) → verify size displayed as "500 bytes"
Functional: Upload file <1 MB (345 KB) → verify size displayed as "345 KB"
Functional: Upload file ≥1 MB (1.2 MB) → verify size displayed as "1.2 MB"
Functional: Upload file ≥10 MB (15.8 MB) → verify size displayed as "15.8 MB" (no limit enforced)
Visual: Verify file size displayed in file card (below filename or in footer)
Functional: Click "Remove" button on file card → verify file card fades out and disappears
Functional: Remove file → verify grid layout reflows automatically (remaining cards adjust positions)
Functional: Remove file → verify file data cleared from memory (no memory leaks)
Visual: Verify remove button visible on hover (desktop) or always visible (mobile)
Interaction: Try to remove file in "processing" state → verify confirmation dialog appears ("This file is currently processing. Are you sure you want to remove it?")
Edge Case: Remove all files → verify empty state reappears (drop zone returns)
Initial State: Load page → verify drop zone displayed with empty state message ("No files uploaded yet. Drag & drop files to get started.")
Initial State: Verify upload icon and friendly illustration visible
Interaction: Upload files → verify drop zone collapses or hides
Interaction: Remove all files → verify drop zone reappears (return to initial state)
Visual: Verify empty state includes visual cue (upload icon, clear instructions)
Edge Case: Upload 100+ files → verify grid handles large number without performance issues (less than 2 seconds card creation)
Edge Case: Upload 0-byte file → verify file size shows "0 bytes"
Edge Case: Upload file with very long name (100+ chars) → verify truncated correctly (max 30 chars, full name on hover)
Edge Case: Upload file with special characters in name (é, ñ, 中文) → verify displays correctly
Edge Case: Drag file outside drop zone → verify highlight disappears (dragleave event)
Edge Case: Drag non-file item (text, image) → verify handled gracefully (no crash)
Browser Compatibility: Test drag-drop on mobile Safari (iOS) → may not work, verify file picker fallback works
Browser Compatibility: Test drag-drop on Android Chrome → verify works correctly
Accessibility: Tab through upload section → verify drop zone and "Browse Files" button keyboard accessible
Accessibility: Verify touch targets ≥44x44px on mobile (drop zone, "Browse Files" button, remove buttons)
